title: Act on the Conditions for Operating Vehicles on Roads

parts.REGISTR A REGISTRACE SILNIČNÍHO VOZIDLA: REGISTER AND REGISTRATION OF A ROAD VEHICLE
parts.Změna zákona o pojištění odpovědnosti z: Amendment to the Act on Liability Insurance for Vehicle Operation

chapters.SCHVALOVÁNÍ TECHNICKÉ ZPŮSOBILOSTI JEDNO: APPROVAL OF THE TECHNICAL SUITABILITY OF AN INDIVIDUALLY MANUFACTURED ROAD VEHICLE
chapters.TECHNICKÁ ZPŮSOBILOST JEDNOTLIVĚ DOVEZEN: TECHNICAL SUITABILITY OF AN INDIVIDUALLY IMPORTED ROAD VEHICLE

p1.heading: Subject matter
p1.text:
(1) This Act regulates the following conditions for operating vehicles on roads:

- a) vehicle registration and deregistration of vehicles from the register,

- b) technical requirements for the operation of road vehicles and special vehicles and the approval of their technical suitability for operation on roads,

- c) rights and obligations of persons who manufacture, import, and place vehicles and fuels on the market,

- d) rights and obligations of vehicle owners and operators,

- e) rights and obligations of technical inspection stations and emission measurement stations,

- f) inspections of the technical condition of vehicles in operation.

(2) This Act regulates the exercise of state administration and state supervision in the area of conditions for operating vehicles on roads.

(3) This Act does not apply to military vehicles.^1)

p2.heading: Basic concepts
p2.text:
(1) A road vehicle is a motorized or non-motorized vehicle manufactured for the purpose of operation on roads for the transport of persons, animals, or goods.

(2) A special vehicle is a vehicle manufactured for purposes other than operation on roads, which may be approved for operation on roads if the conditions stipulated by this Act are met.

(3) A trailer is a non-motorized road vehicle intended to be towed by another vehicle, with which it is connected to form a combination.

(4) A historical vehicle or a sports-historical vehicle (hereinafter referred to as a „historical vehicle“) is a vehicle for which the Ministry of Transport and Communications (hereinafter referred to as the „Ministry“) has issued a historical vehicle registration certificate based on international treaties by which the Czech Republic is bound, and for which the Ministry has issued a special registration plate for a historical vehicle.

(5) A vehicle system is any structural system of a vehicle to which technical requirements stipulated by an implementing legal regulation apply. Examples of vehicle systems include brakes or emission reduction devices.

(6) A vehicle component is a part of a vehicle whose type must be approved independently of the vehicle, if so stipulated by an implementing legal regulation, and to which technical requirements stipulated by an implementing legal regulation apply. An example of a vehicle component is a lamp.

(7) A separate technical unit of a vehicle is a part whose type may be approved independently of the vehicle, but only in relation to one type of vehicle or multiple types of vehicles, if so stipulated by an implementing legal regulation, and to which technical requirements stipulated by an implementing legal regulation apply. Examples of a separate technical unit of a vehicle include a rear bumper of a vehicle, a fixed or interchangeable bodywork of a vehicle.

(8) A vehicle category is a group of vehicles that have the same technical conditions stipulated by an implementing legal regulation. The classification of vehicles into categories is provided in the appendix to the Act.

(9) The manufacturer of a vehicle, vehicle system, vehicle component, or separate technical unit of a vehicle is the person responsible for complying with the technical requirements stipulated by this Act and the conditions stipulated in the decision on type approval of the vehicle, vehicle system, vehicle component, or separate technical unit of a vehicle, and for ensuring the conformity of manufactured vehicles with the type of vehicle, vehicle system, vehicle component, separate technical unit of a vehicle, whose type has been approved.

(10) An accredited importer is a legal entity with its registered office in the territory of the Czech Republic, or a natural person with a permanent residence in the territory of the Czech Republic, and registered by the Ministry as a representative of a foreign manufacturer based on their written authorization. The accredited importer imports vehicles of this foreign manufacturer into the territory of the Czech Republic and issues a technical certificate or technical vehicle certificate for these vehicles.

(11) A testing station is a technical inspection station authorized by the Ministry to perform technical inspections of individual vehicles and interchangeable bodywork or small series of vehicles before the approval of their technical suitability for operation on roads.

(12) An administrative inspection of a road vehicle is an inspection of a road vehicle performed by a technical inspection station, consisting of comparing the data stated in the technical certificate of the road vehicle and in the registration certificate of the road vehicle or in the certificate of technical suitability of the vehicle, if it is a vehicle not subject to registration, with the actual data and condition of the vehicle.

(13) The operator of a road vehicle registered in the road vehicle register of the Czech Republic means a natural person with a permanent residence or an authorized residence in the Czech Republic, or a legal entity with its registered office in the Czech Republic, who operates the road vehicle in their own name and is simultaneously the owner of the road vehicle or is authorized by the owner of the road vehicle to operate the road vehicle.

p3.heading: Types of vehicles
p3.text:
(1) Road vehicles are divided into individual types and categories. The division of road vehicles into categories, the technical description of individual categories of road vehicles, and their further classification are stipulated in the annex to this Act.

(2) Road vehicles are divided into the following basic types:

- a) motorcycles,

- b) passenger cars,

- c) buses,

- d) trucks,

- e) special vehicles,

- f) trailers,

- g) other road vehicles.

(3) Special vehicles are divided into the following basic types:

- a) agricultural or forestry tractors and their trailers,

- b) self-propelled working machines,

- c) towed working machines,

- d) non-motorized working machines or non-motorized vehicles towed or pushed by a pedestrian,

- e) wheelchairs with manual or motor drive, if their width or length does not exceed one meter, their design speed does not exceed 6 km.h^-1, or their maximum permissible mass does not exceed 450 kg.

For the purposes of this Act, a special vehicle also means a mobile machine, industrial equipment capable of transport, or a vehicle without a body, in which an internal combustion engine is installed.

p4.heading: Register of Road Vehicles
p4.text:
(1) The Register of Road Vehicles is a record of road motor vehicles, trailers, and the operators of these vehicles.

(2) The Register of Road Vehicles is a list maintained by district authorities. The district authority competent for the registration of a road motor vehicle and a trailer is the district authority in whose territorial district the operator of the road motor vehicle and trailer has their permanent residence or place of business, if different from the permanent residence, or registered office.

(3) Only a person who demonstrates a legal interest is entitled to inspect the Register of Road Vehicles and request a copy or extract of the recorded data from the district authority.

(4) The following are entered into the Register of Road Vehicles:

- a) owner (name, registered office, and identification number, if it is a legal entity or a natural person entrepreneur; name, surname, birth number, place of permanent or permitted residence, or address of residence upon granting asylum, if it is a natural person),

- b) operator, if not simultaneously the owner (name, registered office, and identification number, if it is a legal entity or a natural person entrepreneur; name, surname, birth number, place of permanent or permitted residence, or address of residence upon granting asylum, if it is a natural person),

- c) state registration plate of the road motor vehicle and trailer (hereinafter referred to as „registration plate“), date of assignment and withdrawal of the registration plate,

- d) number of the technical certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer, date and place of its issue,

- e) number of the certificate of registration of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- f) type and category of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- g) manufacturer of the road motor vehicle and trailer, brand (trade name specified by the manufacturer), vehicle type, commercial designation,

- h) identification number of the road motor vehicle and trailer (VIN), if none, then the production number of the chassis of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- i) manufacturer of the chassis of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- j) engine manufacturer, type, power, engine displacement, and fuel,

- k) body manufacturer, type, production number, color, number of seating and standing places, or berths, dimensions of the loading area, volume of the box or tank,

- l) data on the overall dimensions of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- m) maximum technically permissible mass, maximum authorized mass, and operating mass of the road motor vehicle and maximum technically permissible axle load and maximum authorized axle load,

- n) type of coupling device, maximum technically permissible mass of the loaded trailer, maximum authorized mass of the trailer, maximum technically permissible mass of the loaded vehicle combination, and maximum authorized mass of the combination,

- o) date of first registration of the vehicle.

(5) In addition to the data specified in paragraph 4, the following data are also entered into the Register of Road Motor Vehicles and Trailers:

- a) purpose for which the road motor vehicle and trailer are intended,

- b) liens encumbering the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- c) data on liability insurance for damage caused by vehicle operation (hereinafter referred to as „vehicle operation liability insurance“) to the extent of the data specified in the vehicle operation liability insurance document,^2)

- d) record of technical inspections,

- e) record of approval of the technical roadworthiness of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- f) record of performed and approved modifications of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- g) deregistration of the road motor vehicle and trailer from the register.

(6) The Ministry of the Interior, the Ministry of Defence, and intelligence services maintain a register of road vehicles used for their purposes according to this Act. The Ministry of the Interior maintains a register of vehicles used for the purposes of customs authorities according to this Act.

(7) The method of maintaining the register of road motor vehicles and trailers, details of the data entered into the register, the method of marking changes, and the templates of individual forms shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

(8) The district authority uses data from the population register maintained according to a special legal regulation^3) for entering personal data about the owner and operator into the Register of Road Vehicles.

p5.heading: Central Register of Road Vehicles
p5.text:
(1) The Ministry shall maintain a central register of road vehicles, which shall contain data submitted by district authorities, and a register of road vehicles of members of diplomatic missions.^4)

(2) The Ministry shall ensure that the Police of the Czech Republic are provided with information from the central vehicle register in a manner enabling remote and continuous access.

(3) An implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the manner, scope, and deadlines for the submission of data by district authorities to the central register and for the publication of statistics on registered vehicles.

p6.text:
(1) Anyone who intends to operate a road motor vehicle and a trailer in the territory of the Czech Republic is obliged to register these vehicles on the prescribed form with the competent district authority, with the exception of persons whose stay in the territory of the Czech Republic does not exceed 185 days in the relevant calendar year (hereinafter referred to as the „applicant“).

(2) A road motor vehicle and a trailer may be entered in the road vehicle register if:

- a) their technical roadworthiness for operation on roads has been approved, and

- b) liability insurance for vehicle operation^5) concerning such vehicle has been arranged.

(3) The application for registration of a road motor vehicle and a trailer must contain:

- a) information on the type and category of the road motor vehicle and trailer, the make (trade name specified by the manufacturer), the type of the road motor vehicle and trailer, the commercial designation, the number of the technical certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer, and the approval number of the technical roadworthiness of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- b) information on the owner of the road motor vehicle and trailer pursuant to § 4 para. 4 letter a),

- c) information on the operator of the road motor vehicle and trailer, if the owner is not simultaneously the operator of the road motor vehicle and trailer, pursuant to § 4 para. 4 letter b).

(4) The applicant shall attach to the application for registration of a road motor vehicle and a trailer:

- a) the technical certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- b) a document on the technical inspection, if it is a road motor vehicle and trailer that has already been operated,

- c) the owner's consent to register the road motor vehicle and trailer to another operator,

- d) a report on the identification inspection of the road motor vehicle and trailer, if it is not a new vehicle,

- e) a document on vehicle liability insurance,

- f) a document on the lien encumbering the road motor vehicle and trailer, if established,

- g) a document on a residence permit or asylum granted in the territory of the Czech Republic, if the operator of the road motor vehicle and trailer is not a citizen of the Czech Republic,

- h) a document on the deregistration of the vehicle from the vehicle register of another state, if the vehicle was registered in the vehicle register of another state.

p7.heading: Pledge over a road vehicle
p7.text:
(1) When registering a road vehicle, the district authority shall:

- a) assign a registration plate to the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- b) enter into the technical certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer information on the owner of the road motor vehicle and trailer and information on the operator of the road vehicle, if the owner is not simultaneously the operator of the road motor vehicle and trailer, and the registration plate,

- c) issue the applicant with a certificate of registration of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- d) issue a plate with the assigned registration plate.

(2) Upon registration of a road motor vehicle and a trailer, the applicant shall pay an administrative fee to the district authority in accordance with a special act.^6) The operator of the road motor vehicle and trailer is obliged to affix the registration plate to the vehicle in the manner stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

(3) Upon the first assignment of a registration plate, the district authority shall affix a sticker to the registration plate, indicating the date of the regular technical inspection and emission measurement, if the road vehicle is subject to emission measurement.

(4) An implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the model of the technical certificate and the manner of making entries in the technical certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer by the district authority, the model of the certificate of registration of the road motor vehicle and trailer, the form, content, and design of the registration plate, the types of registration plates and the manner of their issuance and handling, the design of the plates with the registration plate and the manner of affixing the plates with the registration plate to the road motor vehicle and trailer, the procedure in case of loss or destruction of the technical certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer, the certificate of registration of the road motor vehicle and trailer, or the registration plate.

p8.text: Unless otherwise provided by this Act, the provisions of the Civil Code and the Commercial Code concerning pledges over movable property shall apply to pledges over road motor vehicles and trailers.

p9.text:
(1) An application for the entry of a pledge over a road motor vehicle and a trailer shall be submitted by the owner of the road vehicle or by the pledgee with the owner's consent. The application must be supported by a contract or a decision on the establishment of the pledge over the road motor vehicle and trailer.

(2) The district authority shall enter the pledge over the road motor vehicle and trailer into the technical certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer. Concurrently, it shall record the pledge in the road vehicle register.

p10.heading: Deletion of pledge entry
p10.text:
The district authority shall delete the pledge from the technical certificate upon the request of the vehicle owner. Concurrently, it shall mark the deletion in the road vehicle register. The request must be supported by a contract or other document on the termination of the pledge over the road motor vehicle and trailer.

Change of data in the road vehicle register

p11.text: The owner of a registered road motor vehicle and trailer, the pledgee, and other persons concerned by an entry in the road vehicle register are obliged to request the relevant district authority to make an entry of changes to the data recorded in the road vehicle register.

p12.heading: Application for entry of changes to data in the road vehicle register
p12.text:
(1) Persons specified in § 11 are obliged to submit an application for entry of changes to data recorded in the road vehicle register within 10 working days from the occurrence of the fact that gives rise to a change in the recorded data.

(2) The application shall be accompanied by

- a) the technical certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- b) the registration certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer,

- c) a decision of the district authority on the approval of technical roadworthiness after the change has been made, in the case of a change to essential parts of the mechanism or construction of the vehicle made in the form of an individual conversion,

- d) other documents certifying the change in the recorded data.

De-registration of a road vehicle from the road vehicle register

p13.text:
(1) The district authority shall decide on the de-registration of a road motor vehicle and trailer from the register if

- a) the road motor vehicle and trailer have such defects in their technical condition that they immediately endanger the safety of road traffic and for which a valid certificate of technical roadworthiness has not been issued by a technical inspection station and an emission measurement station within the period of the regular technical inspection (§ 40), and if this certificate has not been issued even within 6 calendar months from the date of the regular technical inspection,

- b) the road motor vehicle and trailer have ceased to exist,

- c) the liability insurance for the operation of the vehicle concerning the registered vehicle has expired and a new insurance contract for liability insurance for the operation of the vehicle has not been concluded within seven days of the expiry of the liability insurance for the operation of the vehicle.

(2) The district authority shall de-register a road vehicle from the register upon the request of the owner of the road motor vehicle and trailer. The applicant shall submit with the application for de-registration of the road vehicle from the register also a document of its liquidation.

(3) In cases according to paragraph 1 letter a) or c), the district authority shall decide, upon the request of the owner of the road motor vehicle and trailer, on the temporary de-registration of the road vehicle from road traffic for a period not exceeding 12 calendar months; in such a case, the owner of the road vehicle is obliged to submit, together with the application, the registration certificate of the road vehicle, the plate with the assigned registration plate, and present the technical certificate of the road vehicle for the entry of a record of its de-registration from road traffic. The district authority shall record in the road vehicle register data on the beginning and end of the period during which the road vehicle is de-registered from road traffic.

(4) No later than before the expiry of the period of temporary de-registration of the road vehicle, its owner is obliged to prove to the district authority the fulfillment of the conditions for the operation of the vehicle on roads. On the day of fulfillment of these conditions, the district authority shall return to the owner of the vehicle the registration certificate of the road vehicle and the plate with the assigned registration plate and shall record these facts in the vehicle register. In the event of the fruitless expiry of the period of temporary de-registration of the road vehicle, the district authority shall decide on the de-registration of the road vehicle from the register.

p14.text:
(1) Upon de-registration of a road motor vehicle and trailer from the register, the district authority shall carry out

- a) the deletion of the road motor vehicle and trailer from the road vehicle register,

- b) an entry in the technical certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer regarding the deletion of the road motor vehicle and trailer from the road vehicle register.

(2) The owner of the road motor vehicle and trailer is obliged, no later than seven days from the legal force of the decision, if it concerns the de-registration of a road motor vehicle and trailer according to § 13 par. 1, or within seven days from the submission of the application, if it concerns the de-registration of a road motor vehicle and trailer according to § 13 par. 2, to hand over to the district authority the registration certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer, the plate with the assigned registration plate, and to present the technical certificate of the road motor vehicle and trailer for the entry of a record of the de-registration of the road motor vehicle and trailer from the register. The registration plate shall not be handed over to the district authority in the event that the road motor vehicle and trailer were de-registered from operation due to their demise or theft.

p15.text:
(1) A manufacturer or accredited importer (hereinafter referred to as “manufacturer”) who intends to place mass-produced road vehicles, vehicle systems, vehicle components or separate technical units of a vehicle on the market must have a valid certificate of type-approval of the technical fitness of the vehicle for the vehicle and a valid certificate of homologation for vehicle components, vehicle systems and separate technical units of a vehicle.

(2) A type of road vehicle means vehicles of one category that have the same manufacturer, the same make (trade name specified by the manufacturer) and trade designation of the vehicle, and that have essential conceptual and structural features, which are the frame or floor section. A type of road vehicle may include a type variant and a version of a vehicle type variant.

(3) For the purposes of deciding on type-approval by vehicle categories, the Ministry publishes technical regulations, technical standards and technical specifications (hereinafter referred to as “regulatory basis”) and a list of data and technical documentation (hereinafter referred to as “information document”), according to which the type of road vehicle or vehicle system or vehicle component or separate technical unit of a vehicle will be approved.

(4) In the information document, the Ministry shall define from which technical requirements or procedures exceptions may be permitted upon request when deciding on type-approval. Exceptions cannot be permitted from technical requirements relating to

- a) brakes,

- b) external noise,

- c) emission of pollutants in exhaust gases,

- d) vehicle suppression and electromagnetic compatibility.

p16.heading: Type Homologation
p16.text:
(1) The Ministry issues a certificate on the results of the assessment of conformity of the characteristics of a type of road vehicle, a type of vehicle system, a type of vehicle component or a type of separate technical unit of a vehicle with the requirements stipulated for a road vehicle, vehicle system, vehicle component or separate technical unit of a vehicle by regulations issued on the basis of an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound,^7) (hereinafter referred to as “type homologation certificate”).

(2) The Ministry may grant authorization to perform activities related to the issuance of a type homologation certificate to a legal entity that is technically and structurally equipped to perform verification and assessment of conformity of the characteristics of a road vehicle, vehicle system, vehicle component or separate technical unit of a vehicle and that ensures that the natural persons who will perform these activities are professionally qualified.

(3) Employees of the person holding the authorization pursuant to paragraph 2 are obliged to maintain confidentiality regarding the data they become acquainted with during the performance of activities pursuant to paragraph 1. This obligation continues even after the termination of employment. These persons may be released from the obligation to maintain confidentiality only by the person in whose interest they have this obligation, or in the public interest by a senior employee, in writing, stating the scope and purpose.

(4) Type tests required by the Ministry for homologation shall be carried out at the manufacturer's expense.

p17.heading: Application for Type-Approval of Technical Fitness
p17.text:
(1) The Ministry decides on the type-approval of the technical fitness of a type of road vehicle, a type of vehicle system, a type of vehicle component or a type of separate technical unit of a vehicle on the basis of a written application from the manufacturer.

(2) The application must contain

- a) the business name, registered office and identification number, if it is a legal entity, or the name, surname, business name, if it is an entrepreneur, birth number, place of permanent or permitted residence, if it is a natural person,

- b) the trade designation, make (trade name specified by the manufacturer) and type of road vehicle, its systems, component or separate technical unit of the road vehicle,

- c) the purpose for which the road vehicle is to be used,

- d) data on how warranty and post-warranty service is provided.

(3) The application must be accompanied by the following documents:

- a) a certified copy of the contract or document on the establishment or foundation of the legal entity or, for legal entities registered in the commercial register, an extract from the commercial register, for natural persons, a certified copy of the trade license,

- b) an information folder containing data, drawings, photographs and other documents specified in the information document for the design of the type of road vehicle, type of vehicle system, type of vehicle component or type of separate technical unit,

- c) type homologation certificates for vehicle systems, components and separate technical units that constitute the road vehicle,

- d) the road vehicle's operating manual in Czech,

- e) a declaration on the implementation of a quality management and production control system.

(4) If the manufacturer supports the application for type-approval of technical fitness with vehicle type homologation certificates pursuant to § 27 (2), the application does not need to be accompanied by the documents listed in paragraph 3 (a) to (c) and (e).

p18.heading: Multi-stage approval of the technical fitness of a type of road vehicle
p18.text:
(1) If a road vehicle is manufactured in multiple stages by two or more manufacturers, the manufacturer may apply for type-approval of the vehicle in stages, in separate proceedings, according to the state of completion of the road vehicle.

(2) Multi-stage type-approval of a road vehicle may commence with proceedings for type-approval of a basic vehicle. A basic vehicle means an incomplete road vehicle identified by the manufacturer's trade mark, vehicle code, or vehicle identification number (VIN), which shall remain unchanged for subsequent multi-stage type-approval proceedings.

(3) For an application for type-approval of a basic vehicle, § 17 (2) and (3) shall apply mutatis mutandis. In further proceedings, the manufacturer shall supplement the application with

- a) an information folder corresponding to the state of manufacture of the road vehicle, which was not submitted in previous type-approval proceedings for the incomplete (partially completed) road vehicle,

- b) certificates of type-approval for the incomplete (partially completed) road vehicle in previous stages.

(4) Details of multi-stage type-approval shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p19.heading: Conditions for Type-Approval
p19.text:
(1) Type-approval of a road vehicle or a vehicle system, component, or separate technical unit may be granted if the applicant demonstrates

- a) that the type of road vehicle or the type of vehicle system, component, or separate technical unit corresponds to the data specified in the information document and meets the requirements stipulated by the regulatory basis for the type of road vehicle,

- b) the ability to ensure production and effective control through procedures that ensure a quality management system and production control.

(2) For the purpose of deciding on type-approval of a road vehicle or a vehicle system, component, or separate technical unit, the Ministry shall

- a) verify with the manufacturer, at the manufacturer's expense, that the manufacturer meets the prerequisites for conformity of production, is capable of ensuring effective control of such production, and is capable of ensuring production and control by procedures stipulated by the regulatory basis,

- b) arrange, at the manufacturer's expense, for tests and inspections of the road vehicle, and, if appropriate, also for inspection of the assembly of separate technical units of the road vehicle, vehicle system, component, or separate technical unit by a testing laboratory authorized by the Ministry.

(3) The Ministry is entitled to refuse type-approval, even if the road vehicle or vehicle system, component, or separate technical unit and their manufacturer meet the requirements under paragraph 1, in the event that the product has characteristics that demonstrably pose a serious risk to road traffic safety.

(4) Type-approval of vehicle systems, vehicle components, and separate technical units that have been homologated according to § 16 and § 27 (2) shall not be granted, with the exception of mass assembly according to paragraph 2 (b). The Ministry shall issue a certificate of type-approval for a vehicle that has been homologated according to § 27 (2) on the basis of the homologation certificate.

(5) In the event of a positive decision on type-approval of a road vehicle or a vehicle system, component, or separate technical unit, the Ministry shall issue a certificate of type-approval for the road vehicle or vehicle system, component, or separate technical unit. The model of the type-approval certificate shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

(6) The Ministry shall decide on type-approval of a road vehicle or a vehicle system, component, or separate technical unit within a period of no later than 45 days from the date of delivery of the application supplemented with the prescribed documents. In particularly complex cases, the Ministry shall decide no later than 60 days from the date of delivery of the application supplemented with the prescribed documents.

(7) The Ministry may grant authorization to conduct tests and inspections for the purpose of deciding on type-approval of a road vehicle, vehicle system, component, or separate technical unit to a person who meets the conditions stipulated in § 16 (2) and (3) (hereinafter referred to as an „authorized testing laboratory“).

(8) Details of type-approval shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p20.heading: Decision on type-approval of technical fitness
p20.text:
(1) In its decision on type-approval of the technical fitness of a road vehicle, the Ministry shall state:

- a) the business name, registered office and identification number, if the applicant is a legal entity, or the name, surname, business name, birth registration number, place of permanent or permitted residence, if the applicant is a natural person,

- b) the trade name, make (trade name specified by the manufacturer), type of road vehicle and commercial designation,

- c) the purpose for which the road vehicle is to be used,

- d) the method of ensuring warranty and post-warranty service for the road vehicle,

- e) the conditions for the manufacture of the road vehicle,

- f) exceptions from the technical conditions according to this Act (§ 15 (3) and (4)).

(2) In its decision on type-approval of the technical fitness of a vehicle system, vehicle component or separate technical unit of a vehicle, the Ministry shall state the data specified in paragraph 1 (a), (b), (d), (e) and (f) and shall lay down the conditions for their use and the conditions for their installation on the vehicle.

p21.heading: Amendment to the decision on type-approval of technical fitness
p21.text:
(1) The holder of a certificate of type-approval of the technical fitness of a road vehicle or a vehicle system, vehicle component or separate technical unit of a vehicle is obliged to notify the Ministry of any change concerning the data and documents that are stipulated as requirements for an application for type-approval of the technical fitness of a road vehicle or a vehicle system, vehicle component or separate technical unit of a vehicle, on the basis of which the type-approval decision was made, and to submit documents pertaining thereto.

(2) After assessing the changes in the data and documents, the Ministry shall decide on amending the decision on type-approval of a road vehicle or a vehicle system, vehicle component or separate technical unit of a vehicle. If the decision on the amendment requires new tests and inspections, the Ministry shall arrange for these tests and inspections to be carried out by an authorised testing laboratory at the manufacturer's expense. The Ministry shall make the decision within 30 days of the notification of the change.

p22.heading: Withdrawal of a certificate of type-approval of technical fitness
p22.text:
(1) The Ministry shall decide on the withdrawal of a certificate of type-approval of the technical fitness of a road vehicle or a vehicle system, vehicle component or separate technical unit of a vehicle if

- a) the holder of the certificate of type-approval of technical fitness has ceased to meet any of the conditions on the basis of which the certificate of type-approval was granted,

- b) the holder fails to fulfil the obligations stipulated by this Act or by the decision on granting the certificate of type-approval of technical fitness,

- c) road traffic safety so requires.

(2) The Ministry shall withdraw the certificate of type-approval of technical fitness if the holder of the certificate has requested it.

p23.heading: Lapse of validity of a certificate of type-approval of technical fitness
p23.text:
A certificate of type-approval of the technical fitness of a road vehicle or a vehicle system, vehicle component or separate technical unit of a vehicle shall cease to be valid

- a) upon the expiry of the period for which the certificate of type-approval of technical fitness was granted,

- b) on the date of dissolution of the legal entity that is the holder of the certificate of type-approval of technical fitness,

- c) upon the expiry of 30 days from the death of the natural person to whom the certificate of type-approval of technical fitness was granted,

- d) on the date on which the Ministry's decision on the withdrawal of the certificate of type-approval of technical fitness became final.

Manufacturer's obligations

p25.text:
(1) For road vehicles intended for placing on the market in the Czech Republic, which are subject to registration under this Act, the manufacturer shall issue a technical certificate of the road vehicle, indicate therein data on conformity with the approved type of road vehicle and the technical description of the vehicle. For road vehicles not subject to registration, the manufacturer shall issue a technical certificate of the road vehicle.

(2) The manufacturer of a component or a separate technical unit is obliged to place on each manufactured component or separate technical unit manufactured in conformity with the approved type the designation of the trade name or brand or type mark or number, if required by the implementing legal regulation.

p26.heading: Production supervision
p26.text:
(1) The Ministry shall supervise the manufacture of road vehicles, vehicle systems, vehicle components or separate technical units of vehicles for which the Ministry has issued a type-approval certificate. If it finds that the manufacturer is violating the obligations stipulated by this Act and the type-approval certificate during manufacturing, it is entitled, according to the nature of the deficiencies found, to impose on the manufacturer the method and deadline for rectifying these deficiencies and their causes. Other rights and obligations in the exercise of supervision over manufacturing are stipulated by a special legal regulation.^8)

(2) If the life or health of persons or the safety of road traffic could be immediately endangered, the Ministry is entitled to immediately suspend production, withhold the type-approval certificate for a road vehicle or vehicle system, vehicle component or separate technical unit. General regulations on administrative proceedings do not apply to the issuance of this measure. The Ministry shall initiate proceedings for the withdrawal of the type-approval certificate within 10 days from the date of withholding the type-approval certificate.

p27.heading: Recognition of a road vehicle type-approval certificate issued by another state
p27.text:
(1) The Ministry shall recognize a valid type-approval certificate for a vehicle, vehicle system, vehicle component or separate technical unit of a vehicle issued by another state on the basis of an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound.^7)

(2) The Ministry shall recognize a type-approval certificate for a road vehicle type, vehicle system type, vehicle component type and separate technical unit type, which was issued by a European Union Member State according to regulations issued by the European Community (hereinafter referred to as „EC type-approval certificate“).

(3) On the basis of a written application from the manufacturer for type-approval, the Ministry shall recognize a road vehicle type-approval certificate which was issued by a European Union Member State according to regulations valid in that state, in the event that the vehicle meets the technical conditions stipulated by this Act, and shall issue a decision on type-approval.

(4) For the recognition of road vehicle type-approval certificates, Part Three shall apply, with the exception of § 16, § 17 para. 3 letters b) and e), § 19 para. 2 and para. 6 and § 24.

p28.heading: Road vehicles manufactured in small series
p28.text:
(1) The Ministry may, on the basis of a written application from a manufacturer intending to mass-produce road vehicles in a small series, decide to permit an exemption from the information document stipulated for the type-approval of a motor vehicle. The provisions of § 17 para. 2 and 3 apply to the particulars of the application. In addition, the application must state what exemption is to be permitted and its justification.

(2) Mass production in a small series means the production or import of no more than 500 units of road vehicles manufactured or imported during one calendar year.

p29.text:
(1) The manufacture of an individual road vehicle means the manufacture of no more than five units of one type of road vehicle according to one's own design or using a vehicle system, vehicle component and separate technical unit of a vehicle for which type-approval decisions have been issued.

(2) The manufacturer must apply in writing to the district authority in advance for permission to manufacture an individual motor vehicle. The district authority competent to issue the permit is the one in whose territorial district the manufacturer has its registered office or residence or place of business, if different from the residence.

(3) In the information document, the Ministry shall define from which technical requirements or procedures exemptions may be permitted when deciding on the technical eligibility of an individually manufactured road vehicle. Exemptions cannot be permitted from technical requirements relating to

- a) brakes,

- b) external noise,

- c) exhaust gas emissions of pollutants,

- d) vehicle suppression and electromagnetic compatibility.

p30.heading: Application for permission to manufacture an individual road vehicle
p30.text:
(1) The manufacturer's application for permission to manufacture an individual road vehicle must contain

- a) the business name, registered office and identification number, if the applicant is a legal entity, or the name, surname, business name, if it is an entrepreneur, birth number, place of permanent or permitted residence, if the applicant is a natural person,

- b) the type and category of the road vehicle,

- c) the purpose for which the road vehicle is to be used,

- d) the method of ensuring warranty and post-warranty service.

(2) The application must be accompanied by the following documents:

- a) a certified copy of the contract or document on the establishment or founding of a legal entity or, for legal entities registered in the commercial register, an extract from the commercial register, for natural person entrepreneurs, a certified copy of the trade license,

- b) a technical description of the road vehicle to the extent of the data stated in the vehicle's technical certificate, including data on the expected operational, driving and dynamic characteristics,

- c) a drawing of the road vehicle assembly with dimensions and weights indicated,

- d) a maintenance and operating manual for the vehicle in Czech,

- e) type-approval certificates for vehicle systems, components and separate technical units that constitute the road vehicle, or a technical protocol.

Decision on the technical eligibility of an individually manufactured road vehicle

p31.text:
(1) The technical roadworthiness of an individually manufactured road vehicle shall be approved by the district authority that issued the permit for the manufacture of the individual road motor vehicle. The district authority shall decide on the basis of an assessment of the conformity of the properties of the individually manufactured road vehicle with the requirements stipulated by the implementing legal regulation. The district authority may order the performance of tests of the individually manufactured road vehicle at the manufacturer's expense.

(2) An application for the approval of the technical roadworthiness of an individually manufactured road vehicle shall be subject to § 30. The application must also be accompanied by a technical report issued by a testing station. The technical report shall demonstrate the fulfilment of the conditions imposed in the decision on the manufacturing permit and the fulfilment of the technical requirements stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

(3) The district authority shall decide no later than within 60 days from the date of delivery of the manufacturer's written application supported by the prescribed documents.

p32.text:
The district authority shall state in the decision on the technical roadworthiness of an individually manufactured road vehicle:

- a) the business name, registered office and identification number, if the applicant is a legal entity, or the first name, surname, business name, if the applicant is an entrepreneur, birth number, place of permanent or permitted residence, if the applicant is a natural person,

- b) the type and category of the road vehicle,

- c) the purpose for which the road vehicle is to be used,

- d) the method of ensuring warranty and post-warranty service of the road vehicle.

p33.text:
(1) In the event of a positive decision on the approval of the technical roadworthiness of an individually manufactured road vehicle, the district authority shall issue a technical certificate of the road vehicle, if it is a road vehicle subject to registration. For road vehicles not subject to registration, the district authority shall issue a technical certificate of the road vehicle.

(2) The specimen of the technical certificate and the technical certificate of the road vehicle shall be stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

p34.text:
(1) The technical roadworthiness of an individually imported road vehicle shall be approved by the district authority on the basis of a written application. The competent district authority is the one in whose territorial district the applicant has residence or registered office.

(2) An application for the approval of the technical roadworthiness of an individually imported road vehicle must contain:

- a) the business name, registered office and identification number, if the applicant is a legal entity, or the first name, surname, business name, if the applicant is an entrepreneur, birth number, place of permanent or permitted residence, if the applicant is a natural person,

- b) the type and category of the road vehicle, the manufacturer of the road vehicle, the make (trade name specified by the manufacturer), the vehicle type and the commercial designation of the vehicle,

- c) the purpose for which the road vehicle is to be used.

(3) The application must be supported by the following documents:

- a) a certificate of approval of the technical roadworthiness of a road motor vehicle issued by another state or its certified copy,

- b) a document on the deregistration of the road vehicle from the register of another state, if it is a vehicle that has already been registered in another state,

- c) a report on the registration inspection, if it is a vehicle that has already been registered in another state,

- d) a technical report issued by a testing station, if it is a vehicle that has not been registered in another state, or a vehicle for which a period longer than five years has not elapsed since its first registration in another state,

- e) a technical report issued by an authorised testing laboratory, if it is a vehicle that has been imported from a European Union state and for which a period longer than five years has elapsed since its first registration in that state,

- f) a vehicle maintenance and operation manual in the Czech language.

p35.text:
(1) The district authority shall decide on the approval of the technical roadworthiness of an individually imported road vehicle if the applicant proves that:

- a) an EC type-approval certificate has been granted for the road vehicle,

- b) no structural changes have been made to the road vehicle,

- c) the data in the submitted documents for the application correspond to the facts ascertained by the registration inspection report.

(2) The district authority shall decide on the approval of the technical roadworthiness of an individually imported road vehicle for which the granting of an EC type-approval certificate is not proven and:

- a) which has not yet been registered, or

- b) for which a period longer than five years has not elapsed since its first registration in another state,

on the basis of a technical report issued by a testing station. The testing station shall issue a technical report if the vehicle meets the technical requirements stipulated by a legal regulation or an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound, and which were valid for the given category of vehicle in the Czech Republic at the time of vehicle manufacture.

(3) The district authority shall decide no later than within 30 days from the date of delivery of the written application supported by the prescribed documents.

(4) In the event of a positive decision on the approval of the technical roadworthiness of an individually imported road vehicle, the district authority shall issue a technical certificate of the road vehicle, if it is a road vehicle subject to registration. For road vehicles not subject to registration, the district authority shall issue a technical certificate of the road vehicle.

(5) In an information document, the Ministry may define which technical requirements or procedures may be subject to exceptions upon request when deciding on the approval of the technical roadworthiness of an individually imported road vehicle. Exceptions cannot be granted for technical requirements concerning:

- a) brakes,

- b) external noise,

- c) exhaust emissions of pollutants,

- d) vehicle suppression and electromagnetic compatibility.

p36.text:
(1) Only such a road vehicle that is technically fit for operation on roads in accordance with this Act may be operated on roads.

(2) For trial operation, a road vehicle may be operated on roads only on the basis of a permit issued by the Ministry after verifying the conditions stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

(3) A historic vehicle may be operated on roads only on the basis of a historic vehicle registration certificate and with a special registration plate for a historic vehicle. A sports vehicle may be operated only on the basis of a permit issued by the Ministry. The method and conditions of registration and other details regarding the operation of historic and sports vehicles shall be stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

(4) The operator of a road vehicle is obliged to maintain the vehicle in proper technical condition according to the operating and maintenance instructions stipulated by the manufacturer.

(5) The technical condition of road vehicles in operation on roads is authorized to be inspected by the Police of the Czech Republic within the scope of road traffic safety supervision according to a special Act.^9)

p37.heading: Road vehicle technically unfit for operation
p37.text:
A road vehicle is technically unfit for operation on roads if

- a) due to defects in its technical condition, it immediately endangers the safety of road traffic,

- b) it damages the environment beyond the extent stipulated by the implementing legal regulation,

- c) the vehicle operator fails to prove its technical fitness for operation on roads in the manner stipulated by this Act,

- d) unapproved changes or interventions into vehicle identifiers, such as VIN, have been made to the vehicle.

p38.heading: Road motor vehicles and their trailers in operation
p38.text:
(1) The operator of a road motor vehicle and a trailer must not operate on roads a vehicle,

- a) which is technically unfit for operation,

- b) which is not registered in the road vehicle register in the Czech Republic or in the road vehicle register of another state,

- c) which is not equipped with a registration plate or a registration plate of another state,

- d) for which liability insurance for vehicle operation has not been arranged,^5)

- e) which does not have a valid certificate of technical fitness issued by an emission measurement station and a technical inspection station,

- f) which does not have identification data consistent with the data listed in the road vehicle register.

(2) The operator of a road motor vehicle and a trailer must, when travelling abroad, place the international distinguishing mark of the Czech Republic on the road motor vehicle. The international distinguishing mark of the Czech Republic contains the designation CZ.

p39.text: The operator of a road vehicle is obliged, at their own expense, to present the road vehicle for a regular technical inspection and the road motor vehicle also for regular emission measurement within the deadlines stipulated by this Act.

p40.heading: Regular technical inspections
p40.text:
(1) The operator of a road vehicle shall present for a technical inspection

- a) a passenger car, a truck whose permissible mass does not exceed 3,500 kg, a motorcycle, a trailer whose permissible mass does not exceed 3,500 kg, except for an unbraked trailer whose permissible mass does not exceed 750 kg, no later than four years after the first entry of the road vehicle into the road vehicle register (hereinafter referred to as „registration of a road vehicle“) and then regularly no later than every two years,

- b) a truck whose permissible mass exceeds 3,500 kg, a special purpose vehicle, a bus, a road vehicle with right of way, a driving school training vehicle, a taxi vehicle, a car rental vehicle intended for rental, except for an unbraked trailer whose permissible mass does not exceed 750 kg, a trailer whose permissible mass exceeds 3,500 kg, no later than one year after the registration of the road vehicle and then regularly no later than annually,

- c) an unbraked trailer whose permissible mass does not exceed 750 kg, a motorcycle whose piston internal combustion engine displacement for road vehicle propulsion does not exceed 50 cm^3 or whose maximum design speed does not exceed 50 km.h^-1, with the exception of a motorcycle equipped with pedals, no later than six years after the registration of the road vehicle and then regularly no later than every four years.

(2) The operator of an imported road vehicle that was registered abroad shall present the road vehicle for a technical inspection before the registration of the road vehicle in the Czech Republic and then regularly no later than within the deadlines stipulated in paragraph 1 according to the type of road vehicles, with the exception of a road vehicle listed in paragraph 1 letter a), which the operator of the road vehicle shall present after the first technical inspection regularly no later than every two years.

p41.heading: Regular emission measurement
p41.text: The operator of a road motor vehicle listed in § 40, with the exception of a motorcycle whose operating mass is less than 400 kg, and an electric vehicle, shall present this road vehicle for regular emission measurement one month before the expiry of the technical inspection validity within the deadlines stipulated for its regular technical inspection.

p42.heading: Emission measurement
p42.text: The deadline for presenting a road vehicle for a regular technical inspection and for regular emission measurement is calculated from the date of registration of the road vehicle with the relevant district authority and then always from the date of the regular technical inspection recorded in the technical certificate of the road motor vehicle and the regular emission measurement recorded in the emission measurement certificate or from the date of the statutory obligation to present the vehicle for a regular technical inspection and regular emission measurement.

p43.text: Emission measurement means the inspection of the technical condition of parts of a road motor vehicle, engine and accessories affecting the formation of harmful emissions in exhaust gases, the measurement of parameter values and properties describing the emission behavior of a road motor vehicle, their adjustment and, if necessary, the rectification of identified defects.

p44.text:
(1) For road motor vehicles with spark-ignition engines with uncontrolled emission systems, including systems with uncontrolled catalytic converters, the inspection of the technical condition of parts of a road motor vehicle, engine and accessories affecting the formation of harmful emissions in exhaust gases means:

- a) inspection of the condition of groups and parts affecting exhaust gas emissions and environmental pollution,

- b) inspection of the parameters of the basic engine setting, warmed up to operating temperature, at idle and at increased revolutions, including the measurement of carbon monoxide content in the exhaust gas with simultaneous measurement of unburnt hydrocarbon content.

(2) For road motor vehicles with spark-ignition engines with controlled catalytic systems, the inspection of the technical condition of parts of a road motor vehicle, engine and accessories affecting the formation of harmful emissions in exhaust gases means:

- a) inspection of the condition of groups and parts affecting exhaust gas emissions and environmental pollution,

- b) inspection of the function of the engine control system,

- c) inspection of the carbon monoxide content in the exhaust gas at idle and at increased revolutions and inspection of the lambda air excess coefficient value at increased revolutions for an engine warmed up to operating temperature.

(3) For road motor vehicles with spark-ignition engines with controlled catalytic systems and an EOBD on-board diagnostic system, the inspection of the technical condition of parts of a road motor vehicle, engine and accessories affecting the formation of harmful emissions in exhaust gases means:

- a) inspection of the condition of groups and parts affecting exhaust gas emissions and environmental pollution,

- b) inspection of the function of the EOBD control system,

- c) inspection of the carbon monoxide content in the exhaust gas at idle and at increased revolutions and inspection of the lambda air excess coefficient value at increased revolutions for an engine warmed up to operating temperature.

(4) For road motor vehicles with compression-ignition engines, the inspection of the technical condition of parts of a road motor vehicle, engine and accessories affecting the formation of harmful emissions in exhaust gases means:

- a) inspection of the condition of groups and parts affecting exhaust gas emissions and environmental pollution,

- b) inspection of the engine setting warmed up to operating temperature, especially idle and maximum revolutions; for engines with a controlled emission system, also inspection of the function of the engine control system,

- c) measurement of smoke opacity by the free acceleration method.

(5) Before emission measurement, the documents of the registered vehicle and the emission measurement certificate are checked. The identification data of the vehicle and engine, homologation plates and the correctness of the emission measurement data are verified.

(6) Emission measurement is carried out using methods and instruments and equipment specified by the implementing legal regulation. Emission measurement instruments, specified by the implementing legal regulation, are approved by the Ministry on the basis of tests carried out by an authorized testing laboratory. The instruments must be metrologically traceable.

p45.heading: Assessment and Marking of Emission Measurement
p45.text:
(1) During emission measurement of a road motor vehicle, the emission measurement station assesses whether the parts of the road motor vehicle, engine and accessories affecting the formation of harmful emissions are complete, undamaged, functional and without obvious defects, and whether the values of parameters and properties of the emission behavior of the road vehicle, after the emission measurement has been carried out and even after any adjustment and rectification of identified defects, do not exceed the limits set by the implementing legal regulation.

(2) The emission measurement station shall draw up an emission measurement report for the road motor vehicle on the emission measurement carried out and hand it over to the vehicle operator.

(3) In the event of a positive emission measurement result, the emission measurement station shall issue an emission measurement certificate to the operator of the road motor vehicle, mark the positive emission measurement result therein, and place a control sticker on the rear registration plate indicating the month and year of the next emission measurement.

(4) The emission measurement certificate is an appendix to the road vehicle's technical certificate, which contains:

- a) identification data of the road motor vehicle and its engine, only in case of change,

- b) values of the basic setting and limits of the engine's emission behavior parameters,

- c) the date of the next emission measurement.

(5) If the values of the parameters and properties of the emission behavior of the road motor vehicle after the emission measurement exceed the limits set by the manufacturer and the implementing legal regulation, the road motor vehicle is technically unfit for further operation on roads (§ 37). The emission measurement station shall remove the control sticker from the road vehicle's registration plate.

(6) The types of instruments, the method of metrological assurance, the procedure for marking emission measurements, the model of the emission measurement certificate and the control sticker, the method of assessing the technical condition of parts of the road motor vehicle, engine and accessories during emission measurement shall be determined by the implementing legal regulation.

p46.text:
(1) The operator of a road motor vehicle is obliged to present the emission measurement certificate:

- a) to the emission measurement station during regular emission measurement,

- b) to the technical inspection station before the regular technical inspection of the road motor vehicle.

(2) The driver of a road motor vehicle is entitled to be present during emission measurement at the emission measurement station.

p47.heading: Technical inspection
p47.text:
(1) A „technical inspection“ of a road vehicle means a check of the technical condition, operation of components and equipment of the road vehicle.

(2) A check of the technical condition of a road vehicle means a check of

- a) the braking system,

- b) the steering,

- c) axles, wheels, tires, suspension, shafts, joints,

- d) the chassis and bodywork,

- e) lighting devices and light signaling,

- f) other components and equipment, in particular electrical equipment and wiring, speedometer and tachograph, fuel system, engine and gearbox tightness, clutch, gear shifting, heating and ventilation system, coupling device, exhaust system, radio interference suppression, noise,

- g) prescribed and special equipment.

(3) At the start of a technical inspection, a road motor vehicle must have a positive emission measurement report presented, and a control sticker indicating the validity period of the performed measurement must be affixed to the rear registration plate.

(4) Devices for performing technical inspections specified by an implementing legal regulation shall be approved by the Ministry on the basis of tests carried out by an authorized testing laboratory. The devices must be metrologically verified and traceable.

(5) The driver of a road motor vehicle is entitled to be present during the technical inspection at the technical inspection station.

Assessment of the technical condition and technical roadworthiness of a road motor vehicle and marking of the technical inspection result

p48.text:
(1) During a technical inspection of a road vehicle, the technical inspection station ascertains whether the technical condition and operation of the vehicle's components and parts are free of defects or have defects by comparing the actual technical condition of the vehicle with the conditions stipulated for the vehicle's technical condition by this Act and the implementing legal regulation.

(2) The technical inspection station must not adjust or repair a road vehicle during a regular technical inspection, with the exception of simple headlight adjustment, if the condition and accessibility of the adjustment elements allow it.

(3) The technical inspection station shall draw up a technical inspection report on the regular technical inspection of the road vehicle, hand it over to the vehicle operator, and notify the relevant district authority of the vehicle's technical inspection result.

(4) The scope and method of performing regular technical inspections, the technical conditions for assessing the technical inspection result, and the method of marking the performance of technical inspections shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p49.text:
During a technical inspection of a road vehicle at a technical inspection station, the following degrees of defects in the technical condition of the vehicle, its components and parts may be found:

- a) a minor defect, which does not affect road safety,

- b) a serious defect, which affects the vehicle's operational characteristics and adversely impacts the environment, but does not immediately endanger the safety of vehicle operation or road safety,

- c) a dangerous defect, which immediately endangers the safety of road vehicle operation or road safety.

p50.text:
(1) A road vehicle is technically roadworthy if no defects or only minor defects were found during the technical inspection of the road vehicle, its components and parts.

(2) The technical inspection station shall mark the day, month, and year of the regular technical inspection of the road vehicle by an entry in the vehicle's technical certificate. The technical inspection station shall affix a control sticker on the rear registration plate of the road vehicle, indicating the technical roadworthiness inspection, with the month and year of the next regular technical inspection of the road vehicle.

p51.text:
(1) If a serious defect is found during the technical inspection of a road vehicle, the vehicle is technically roadworthy only for a period of 30 days from the date of marking the entry of the technical inspection result in the vehicle's technical certificate. The operator of the road vehicle is obliged, within this period, to present the road vehicle with the serious defect removed to the same technical inspection station that found this serious defect, for an inspection of the method of removing the serious defect.

(2) In the event of a serious defect being found, § 50 (2) applies mutatis mutandis to the procedure of the technical inspection station.

(3) If the operator of the road vehicle does not proceed in the manner specified in paragraph 1, or if this defect is found repeatedly during the inspection of the method of removing the serious defect, the vehicle is technically unroadworthy (§ 37) and must not be used in operation. The operator is obliged to arrange for its towing from the technical inspection station at their own expense.

p52.text:
(1) If a dangerous defect is found during the technical inspection of a road vehicle, the vehicle is technically unroadworthy and must not be used in operation. The operator is obliged to arrange, at their own expense,

- a) the towing of the vehicle from the technical inspection station,

- b) the removal of the dangerous defect,

- c) the presentation of the road vehicle for a repeated technical inspection.

(2) Upon finding a dangerous defect, the technical inspection station shall remove the control sticker indicating the technical roadworthiness of the road vehicle from the rear registration plate and make an entry of the technical inspection result in the vehicle's technical certificate.

(3) If the operator of the road vehicle presents the road vehicle with the dangerous defect removed to the technical inspection station that found this dangerous defect, within 30 calendar days of its discovery, the technical inspection station shall only perform a check of the removal of the dangerous defect.

p53.text:
(1) The production and distribution of forms for vehicle technical inspection reports, control stickers for the technical roadworthiness of motor vehicles, vehicle emission measurement reports, emission measurement certificates, and vehicle emission measurement control stickers shall be ensured by the Ministry through authorized organizations.

(2) The templates for the vehicle technical inspection report form, the vehicle emission measurement report, the emission measurement certificate and the method of its completion, as well as the template for the vehicle technical roadworthiness control sticker and the vehicle emission measurement control sticker and the method of their completion and placement on the registration plate, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p54.heading: Authorization to operate a technical inspection station
p54.text:
(1) A "technical inspection station" is a workplace specialized in carrying out technical inspections of road vehicles.

(2) A technical inspection station may be operated by a legal or natural person who holds an authorization for its operation granted by the district authority. The district authority competent to decide on the granting of authorization is the district authority in whose territorial district the operator of the technical inspection station will carry out its activities.

(3) The district authority shall grant the applicant authorization to operate a technical inspection station under the following conditions:

- a) the applicant, the statutory body or members of the statutory body of the legal person have reached the age of 18, are legally competent, of good character, and at least one member of the statutory body is professionally qualified,

- b) the applicant must not be legally or economically linked to the manufacture, sale or repair of vehicles or their parts in such a way that they simultaneously operate the manufacture or repair of vehicles or their parts for which they are applying for authorization to operate a technical inspection station, for the entire period of operation of the technical inspection station,

- c) the applicant has parking areas, operational and technical equipment necessary for the operation of the technical inspection station,

- d) the applicant shall ensure that inspections at the technical inspection station are carried out only by persons who hold a professional certificate of a control technician.

(4) For the purposes of this Act, a person shall not be considered of good character if they have been finally convicted of an intentionally committed criminal offense to an unconditional sentence of imprisonment of at least one year, for an intentionally committed criminal offense whose factual basis is related to business, or for a negligently committed criminal offense whose factual basis is related to the subject of business, unless they are deemed not to have been convicted.

(5) The applicant shall demonstrate professional qualification by a valid professional certificate of professional qualification of a control technician (hereinafter referred to as “professional certificate of a control technician”) (§ 60), held by at least one member of the statutory body.

(6) The types of technical inspection stations, basic technical equipment and its arrangement, and the method of its metrological assurance shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p55.heading: Application for authorization to operate a technical inspection station
p55.text:
(1) An application for authorization to operate a technical inspection station must contain

- a) the business name, registered office and identification number, if it is a legal person, or the name, surname, business name, if it is an entrepreneur, birth number, place of permanent residence, if it is a natural person,

- b) the type of technical inspection station,

- c) the premises of the technical inspection station,

- d) the anticipated date of commencement of operation.

(2) The applicant shall attach to the application

- a) a description of the building, access roads and parking lot,

- b) a list of the technological equipment of the inspection line,

- c) a positive statement from the building authority, in whose territorial district the technical inspection station is to be operated, regarding the intention to operate a technical inspection station from the perspective of the land-use plan, environmental protection and, where applicable, other public interest,

- d) a valid professional certificate of a control technician (§ 60), held by at least one member of the statutory body,

- e) a declaration by the applicant that they are not legally or economically linked to the manufacture, sale or repair of vehicles or their parts in such a way that they simultaneously operate the manufacture or repair of vehicles or their parts for which they are applying for authorization to operate a technical inspection station,

- f) an extract from the Criminal Register, not older than three months, for all natural persons who are the statutory body or members of the statutory body, and persons who will carry out inspections at the technical inspection station.

p56.heading: Decision on authorization to operate a technical inspection station
p56.text:
The district authority shall state in the decision on granting authorization to operate a technical inspection station

- a) the business name, registered office and identification number, if it is a legal person, or the name, surname, business name, if it is an entrepreneur, birth number, place of permanent residence, if it is a natural person,

- b) the type of technical inspection station,

- c) the address of the premises of the technical inspection station,

- d) the date of commencement of operation,

- e) the scope of technical inspections to be carried out,

- f) other conditions for commencing the activity of operating the technical inspection station.

p57.text:
(1) A technical inspection station must have a certificate by the date of commencement of operation. The certificate shall be issued by the district authority that decided on the granting of authorization to operate the technical inspection station, based on the application of the operator of the technical inspection station.

(2) With the certificate, the operator of the technical inspection station demonstrates that

- a) they meet the conditions of professional qualification of the persons ensuring the operation of the technical inspection station,

- b) they meet the conditions stipulated by this Act for the operation of the technical inspection station,

- c) they have an internal organizational structure and management system for ensuring technical inspections at the technical inspection station.

(3) The method of verifying compliance with the conditions for operating a technical inspection station shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p58.heading: Obligations of the operator of a technical inspection station
p58.text:
(1) The operator of a "technical inspection station" is obliged to ensure, for the entire period of operation of the technical inspection station, that

- a) vehicle technical inspections are carried out by persons who hold a professional certificate of a control technician,

- b) the technical equipment and arrangement of the technical inspection station are in accordance with the decision issued pursuant to § 56 and the implementing legal regulation,

- c) instruments and equipment used for carrying out vehicle technical inspections are approved and metrologically traceable according to a special legal regulation.

(2) The operator of a technical inspection station is obliged to notify the district authority of changes concerning the data and documents that are stipulated as requirements for an application pursuant to § 55 (1) and (2).

p59.heading: Withdrawal of authorization to operate a technical inspection station
p59.text:
The district authority shall withdraw the authorization to operate a technical inspection station if

- a) during the performance of technical inspections, the obligations stipulated by this Act are seriously violated,

- b) the Ministry, within the scope of state expert supervision, has ordered the cessation of technical inspections.

Professional certificate of a control technician

p60.text:
(1) Technical inspections of vehicles at a technical inspection station may be carried out by persons who hold a professional certificate of a control technician. The Ministry decides on the issuance of a professional certificate of a control technician. In case of rejection of the application, the Ministry shall issue a decision in accordance with the Administrative Procedure Code.

(2) The Ministry shall issue a professional certificate of a control technician to a person who

- a) has completed full secondary vocational education of a technical field and has professional experience in car repair for at least two years or secondary vocational education of a technical field and professional experience in car repair for at least six years,

- b) has reached the age of 21,

- c) holds a driving license of a group or subgroup of driving license for driving a vehicle, the technical inspections of which will be carried out at the technical inspection station,

- d) has completed training consisting of theoretical preparation and practical training on the inspection lines of a technical inspection station,

- e) has passed the final examination of professional competence to carry out technical inspections,

- f) is of good character; for the purposes of this Act, a person who has been finally convicted of an intentionally committed criminal offense to an unconditional prison sentence of at least one year, for an intentionally committed criminal offense whose factual basis is related to business, or for a negligently committed criminal offense whose factual basis is related to the subject of business, unless he is considered as not having been convicted, is not considered to be of good character.

(3) In the professional certificate of a control technician, the Ministry shall state the scope of competence to carry out technical inspections at a technical inspection station. The form of the professional certificate of a control technician shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p61.text:
(1) The professional certificate of a control technician is issued for an indefinite period.

(2) The Ministry shall decide on the withdrawal of the professional certificate of a control technician if

- a) its holder has seriously violated obligations during the inspection of road vehicles,

- b) he has ceased to meet the conditions for its issuance.

p62.text:
(1) Training consisting of theoretical preparation and practical training on the inspection lines of a technical inspection station is provided by the Ministry through authorized organizations.
