Final examinations of professional competence to carry out technical inspections are conducted by the Ministry, which shall establish an examination committee for this purpose. Members of the examination committee are appointed by the Ministry from experts in the field of transport. The chairman of the committee is an employee of the Ministry. Training and final examinations are carried out at the applicant's expense.

(2) The curriculum for theoretical preparation and practical training to acquire professional competence for carrying out technical inspections at a technical inspection station, the scope of knowledge required for successful completion of the final examination of professional competence for carrying out technical inspections, the method of conducting, organizing and evaluating the final examination before the examination committee, and the examination rules shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p63.heading: Authorization to operate an emission measurement station
p63.text:
(1) An "emission measurement station" is a workplace specialized in emission measurement, which is authorized by the vehicle manufacturer or the manufacturer of the vehicle system affecting the formation of harmful emissions in vehicle exhaust gases. Emission measurement stations are divided into types of emission measurement stations according to vehicle types and engine types into emission measurement stations for vehicles

- a) powered by spark-ignition engines,

- b) powered by spark-ignition engines and engines converted to run on liquefied petroleum gas or compressed natural gas,

- c) powered by compression-ignition engines and engines converted to run on liquefied petroleum gas or compressed natural gas.

(2) An emission measurement station may be operated by a legal or natural person who has been granted authorization to operate it by the district authority. The district authority in whose territorial district the operator of the emission measurement station will have its registered office is competent to decide on the granting of authorization.

(3) The district authority shall grant the applicant authorization to operate an emission measurement station under the following conditions:

- a) the statutory body or members of the statutory body of the applicant have reached the age of 18, are legally competent and of good character, in the case of a legal person; the natural person has reached the age of 18, is legally competent and of good character,

- b) the applicant has the operational and technical equipment necessary for the operation of the emission measurement station.

(4) For the definition of good character, the provisions of § 54 (4) apply.

(5) The basic technical equipment of individual types of emission measurement stations shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p64.heading: Application for authorization to operate an emission measurement station
p64.text:
(1) An application for the grant of authorization to operate an emission measurement station to the district authority must contain:

- a) the business name, registered office, and identification number, if it is a legal entity, or the name, surname, business name, if it is an entrepreneur, birth number, place of permanent residence, if it is a natural person,

- b) the type of emission measurement station,

- c) the makes (trade name specified by the manufacturer) and types of vehicles for which emission measurements will be performed,

- d) the location where the emission measurement station will be operated,

- e) the anticipated date of commencement of operation.

(2) The applicant shall attach to the application:

- a) a description of the building, access roads, and parking area,

- b) a list of the technological equipment of the emission measurement station,

- c) a positive statement from the building authority in whose territorial district the emission measurement station is to be established, regarding the intention to operate the emission measurement station from the perspective of the territorial plan and environmental protection, or other public interest, as appropriate,

- d) an extract from the Criminal Register, not older than three months, for all natural persons who are the statutory body or members of the statutory body, if the applicant is a legal entity, or an extract from the Criminal Register, not older than three months, for the applicant, if the applicant is a natural person,

- e) a statement from the hygienic service,

- f) a document of authorization from the vehicle manufacturer or the manufacturer of the vehicle system affecting the formation of harmful emissions in vehicle exhaust gases to establish a specialized repair shop.

p65.heading: Decision on authorization to operate an emission measurement station
p65.text:
The district authority shall state in the decision on authorization to operate an emission measurement station:

- a) the business name, registered office, and identification number, if it is a legal entity, or the name, surname, business name, if it is an entrepreneur, birth number, place of permanent residence, if it is a natural person,

- b) the type of emission measurement station,

- c) the types and makes of vehicles for which the emission measurement station is authorized to perform emission measurements,

- d) the location where the emission measurement station will be operated,

- e) the date of commencement of operation,

- f) the scope of emission measurements to be performed,

- g) other conditions for commencing the operation of the emission measurement station.

p66.text:
(1) An emission measurement station must have a certificate by the date of commencement of operation. The certificate is issued by the district authority that decided on the grant of authorization to operate the emission measurement station, based on the application of the operator of the emission measurement station.

(2) With the certificate, the operator of the emission measurement station demonstrates that:

- a) it meets the conditions of professional competence of persons ensuring the operation of the emission measurement station,

- b) it meets the conditions stipulated by this Act for the operation of the emission measurement station,

- c) it has an internal organizational structure and management system for ensuring emission measurements.

(3) The method of verifying the fulfillment of conditions for the operation of an emission measurement station shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p67.heading: Obligations of the operator of an emission measurement station
p67.text:
(1) The operator of an emission measurement station is obliged to ensure, for the entire period of operation of the emission measurement station, that:

- a) vehicle emission measurements are performed by persons who hold a professional certificate of professional competence of a mechanic (hereinafter referred to as „professional mechanic's certificate“) (§ 69),

- b) the technical equipment and arrangement of the type of emission measurement station, including emission measurement areas, handling areas, exhaust gas extraction equipment, ventilation, heating, are in accordance with the decision issued pursuant to § 65 and the implementing legal regulation,

- c) the instruments and equipment used for performing vehicle emission measurements are approved and metrologically traceable according to a special legal regulation.

(2) The operator of an emission measurement station is obliged to notify the district authority of changes concerning the data and documents that are stipulated as requirements for an application pursuant to § 64 para. 1 and 2.

p68.heading: Withdrawal of authorization to operate an emission measurement station
p68.text:
The district authority shall withdraw the authorization to operate an emission measurement station if:

- a) obligations stipulated by this Act are seriously violated during the performance of emission measurements,

- b) the Ministry, within the scope of state expert supervision, ordered the cessation of emission measurements.

Professional mechanic's certificate

p69.text:
(1) Emission measurements at an emission measurement station may be performed by persons who hold a professional mechanic's certificate. The Ministry decides on the issuance of a professional mechanic's certificate. In case of rejection of the application, the Ministry shall issue a decision in accordance with the Administrative Procedure Code.

(2) The Ministry shall issue a professional mechanic's certificate to a person who:

- a) has completed full secondary vocational education of a technical nature and professional practice of at least three years or is trained in a technical field and has professional practice of at least six years,

- b) has completed theoretical training in emission measurement for vehicles powered by spark-ignition engines or vehicles powered by spark-ignition engines and engines modified for liquefied petroleum gas or compressed natural gas propulsion or vehicles powered by compression-ignition engines and engines modified for liquefied petroleum gas or compressed natural gas propulsion,

- c) has passed the final examination of professional competence to perform emission measurements.

(3) In the professional mechanic's certificate, the Ministry shall state the scope of competence to perform emission measurements at an emission measurement station. The form of the professional mechanic's certificate shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p70.text:
(1) The professional mechanic's certificate is issued for an indefinite period.

(2) The Ministry shall decide on the withdrawal of the professional mechanic's certificate if:

- a) its holder has seriously violated obligations during the performance of road vehicle inspections,

- b) he/she has ceased to meet the conditions for its issuance.

p71.text:
(1) The Ministry shall ensure theoretical training through authorized organizations. Final examinations of professional competence for emission measurement shall be conducted by the Ministry, which shall establish an examination board for this purpose. Members of the examination board shall be appointed by the Ministry from experts in the field of transport. The chairman of the board shall be an employee of the Ministry. Training and final examinations shall be conducted at the applicant's expense.

(2) The curriculum for theoretical training to acquire professional competence for emission measurement at an emission measurement station, the scope of knowledge required for successful completion of the final examination of professional competence for emission measurement, the method of conducting, organizing and evaluating the final examination of professional competence before the examination board, and the examination rules shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p72.text:
(1) The Ministry may, on the basis of a public tender, issue authorization to a technical inspection station to carry out technical inspections of individual types of vehicles and interchangeable superstructures or small series of vehicles before their technical roadworthiness is approved, if it meets the following requirements:

- a) it holds authorization to operate a technical inspection station,

- b) it is technically and structurally equipped to carry out technical inspections,

- c) at least one employee holds a valid certificate for carrying out technical inspections of vehicles before their technical roadworthiness is approved,

- d) the activities will be carried out by natural persons who are professionally competent for these activities and have an obligation to maintain state, business or official secrecy.

(2) The Ministry shall decide on the issuance of a certificate for carrying out technical inspections of vehicles before their technical roadworthiness is approved, if the applicant

- a) holds a professional certificate of an inspection technician,

- b) has successfully completed basic or advanced training in theoretical preparation and practical instruction,

- c) has passed the final examination of professional competence.

(3) Details on the types of testing stations, sample forms of authorization according to paragraph 1 and certificates for operating testing stations, and details on the method of acquiring professional competence for carrying out technical inspections of vehicles before their technical roadworthiness is approved, shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p73.text:
(1) A "reconstruction of a road vehicle" means a change or modification of essential parts of the mechanism or structure of an operated road vehicle.

(2) A change of essential parts of the mechanism or structure of a road vehicle shall include:

- a) a change of the type of propulsion, installation of a different type of engine,

- b) a change of the bodywork, vehicle suspension and wheels causing a change in the permissible load,

- c) a change of the type of bodywork or superstructure, for which the purpose and method of use of the road vehicle changes,

- d) a change of the vehicle category.

(3) The maximum permissible mass of a road vehicle may be reduced only in the case of a vehicle reconstruction into a special vehicle for single-purpose use, provided that the vehicle category does not change.

(4) A vehicle reconstruction must not change the vehicle category if the newly created category is subject to stricter technical requirements stipulated by the implementing legal regulation for vehicle brakes, external vehicle noise, pollutant emissions in vehicle exhaust gases, or active and passive safety features.

(5) Vehicle reconstruction by bodywork replacement may be permitted only within one vehicle type series.

(6) If a vehicle is replaced or supplemented with a new or different component or equipment, this component or equipment must meet the conditions stipulated by this Act.

(7) It is not considered a reconstruction of a road vehicle if the vehicle manufacturer declares an essential part of the mechanism or structure of the road vehicle as a spare part for that vehicle.

p74.text:
(1) The reconstruction of a road vehicle registered in the road vehicle register shall be permitted by the competent district authority on the basis of a written application, provided that the conditions for reconstruction stipulated by the implementing legal regulation are met.

(2) Mass reconstruction of a road vehicle type shall be permitted by the Ministry on the basis of a written application, provided that the conditions for mass reconstruction stipulated by the implementing legal regulation are met.

(3) An application for permission to reconstruct a road vehicle or for mass reconstruction of a road vehicle type must contain:

- a) the business name, registered office and legal form of the legal entity and its identification number, if assigned, if the applicant is a legal entity, or the name and surname, residence, business name, birth number and identification number of the natural person, if assigned, if the applicant is a natural person,

- b) the type and category of the road vehicle,

- c) the purpose for which the road vehicle is to be used.

(4) The application must be accompanied by the following documents:

- a) a detailed description of the road vehicle reconstruction,

- b) a proposal for a change in the data recorded in the road vehicle's technical certificate,

- c) a technical description and drawing documentation of the vehicle system, vehicle structural part or separate technical unit of the vehicle, if their type technical suitability has not been approved,

- d) a technical report issued by a testing station and, in the case of mass vehicle reconstruction, by an authorized testing facility.

p75.text:
(1) "Vehicle equipment" means items intended to ensure the operation, maintenance and repairs of the vehicle, or to protect and secure transported persons or cargo from injury, damage or adverse influences of various kinds, or to increase driving comfort and receive or transmit information from the vehicle, for example, a spare wheel, tools, a warning triangle, means for securing cargo, a first-aid kit, an air conditioning unit, a radio receiver.

(2) A "spare part" means items intended to replace the original separate technical unit or structural part of the vehicle. A spare part subject to type approval must meet the requirements stipulated by the regulatory framework.

p76.text:
(1) Equipment may be used if its design and technical condition comply with the technical requirements stipulated by the implementing legal regulation. Equipment designated by the implementing legal regulation must have approved technical roadworthiness.

(2) The approval of the technical roadworthiness of equipment is governed by Part Three of this Act.

p77.heading: Fuels, operating fluids and lubricants
p77.text:
(1) The "fuel" of a motor vehicle means motor gasoline, diesel fuel, liquefied petroleum gases, biofuel, blended fuel, and compressed and liquefied natural gas. The "operating fluid" of a motor vehicle means, in particular, fluid for the vehicle's braking system and fluid for the cooling system for cooling the motor vehicle's engine. "Lubricants" are lubricating oils and lubricating greases to ensure the technical roadworthiness of the motor vehicle for operation.

(2) Only fuels and operating fluids prescribed by the manufacturer of such engines, systems and equipment may be used to power vehicle engines and to fill lubricating, cooling and other vehicle systems and devices. Fuels and operating fluids used in the operation of road motor vehicles must meet the quality requirements stipulated by the implementing legal regulation, special legal regulations,^10) Czech technical standards, and regulations issued on the basis of an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound.^11) Leaded motor gasoline must not be used for the operation of a vehicle on roads.

(3) The sale of leaded motor gasoline in the Czech Republic and the import of leaded motor gasoline into the Czech Republic are prohibited. For the operation of a vehicle whose engine is designed for the use of leaded motor gasoline, unleaded motor gasoline with additive compounds that enable reliable operation of such engines, or the additive compounds themselves, must be available for sale at a filling station. The additive compounds and their dosage shall be stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

(4) The manufacturer and importer of gasoline and diesel fuel for motor vehicles in operation on roads are obliged to ensure that the fuels they sell comply with the quality requirements stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

(5) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the method of tracking and monitoring the quality of fuels in the area of their production, import and sale, and the method of preparing and submitting reports to international bodies and organizations in the area of tracking and monitoring the quality of fuels.

p78.text:
(1) A special vehicle may be operated if its design and technical condition comply with the technical requirements stipulated by the implementing legal regulation and it has approved technical roadworthiness for operation under this Act.

(2) The approval of the technical roadworthiness of a special vehicle for operation is governed by Part Three of this Act.

(3) The implementing legal regulation shall stipulate the individual types of mobile machinery, industrial equipment capable of transport or vehicles without a body, in which an internal combustion engine is built in, the permissible values of pollutant emissions in exhaust gases, and details of the type approval of internal combustion engines, the method of preparing and submitting reports to international bodies and organizations.

p79.text:
(1) For the registration and procedure for approving the technical roadworthiness of agricultural and forestry tractors and their trailers and self-propelled working machines, Part Two and Part Three of this Act shall apply. For the operation of agricultural and forestry tractors and their trailers, Part Four shall also apply, with the exception of § 40 (1).

(2) For special vehicles not subject to vehicle registration in the road vehicle register, the manufacturer shall issue a technical certificate of the special vehicle. For special vehicles registered in the road vehicle register, the manufacturer shall issue a technical certificate of the special vehicle.

(3) The operator of an agricultural or forestry tractor and its trailer shall present this special vehicle for a technical inspection no later than four years after its first registration in the road vehicle register and then regularly no later than every four years.

(4) The technical inspection of agricultural and forestry tractors and their trailers and the emission measurement of agricultural and forestry tractors may be carried out at a location designated by the relevant district authority in a mobile manner.

(5) The specimen of the technical certificate of a special vehicle and the technical certificate of a special motor vehicle and the certificate of registration of a special motor vehicle and a special trailer, and details on the method of carrying out technical inspections and emission measurements in a mobile manner shall be stipulated by the implementing legal regulation.

p81.heading: State expert supervision
p81.text:
(1) State expert supervision in matters of conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads shall be exercised, in accordance with this Act, by the Ministry and district authorities, and, in accordance with a special legal regulation^5), by the Police of the Czech Republic. In exercising state expert supervision, the Ministry and district authorities shall inspect the fulfillment of obligations and conditions stipulated by this Act. They shall ascertain whether persons on whom this Act imposes obligations in matters of conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads (hereinafter referred to as “obligated persons”) fulfill the imposed obligations and whether they comply with the conditions stipulated in decisions issued by the Ministry or district authorities.

(2) Employees of the Ministry and employees of district authorities authorized to exercise state expert supervision (hereinafter referred to as “authorized persons”) shall proceed in this exercise in accordance with a special legal regulation.^8)

(3) The rights and obligations of authorized persons and obligated persons in the exercise of state expert supervision shall be stipulated by a special legal regulation.^8)

p82.heading: Penalties
p82.text:
(1) In exercising state expert supervision, the authorized person shall be obliged to present proof of authorization. The proof of authorization shall contain the first name and surname of the authorized person, their birth number, photograph, substantive and territorial scope of authorization, and period of validity.

(2) If persons authorized to exercise state expert supervision ascertain a breach of obligations stipulated by this Act and other legal regulations to ensure the safety of vehicle operation on roads, they shall, according to the nature and extent of the ascertained deficiencies, impose on the inspected entity the method and deadline for removing their causes.

(3) If a person authorized to exercise state expert supervision ascertains serious defects in the activity of a technical inspection station or in the activity of an emission measurement station, they shall be authorized to order the immediate cessation of technical inspections and technical or registration checks at the technical inspection station or emission measurements at the emission measurement station and to notify the relevant district authority thereof. General regulations on administrative proceedings shall not apply to the issuance of this measure.

(4) A person authorized to exercise state expert supervision may, immediately after carrying out a technical inspection or emission measurement, invite the operator of a road vehicle to present the inspected vehicle, at their own expense, for a repeated partial or complete technical inspection or for emission measurement for the necessary period. The operator of a road vehicle shall be obliged, based on the invitation of the person authorized by state supervision, to present the road vehicle for a repeated technical inspection or for emission measurement. The immediate repeated technical inspection or emission measurement shall be carried out free of charge by a control technician or mechanic under the supervision of the person authorized to exercise state expert supervision.

p83.text:
(1) The Ministry or the district authority, within the scope of its competence, shall impose a fine of up to CZK 50,000,000 on a person who

- a) mass-produced and placed on the market a road vehicle, vehicle system, vehicle component, or separate technical unit of a vehicle and vehicle equipment without a valid type-approval certificate,

- b) mass-produced and placed on the market a road vehicle, vehicle system, vehicle component, or separate technical unit of a vehicle without a decision on the recognition of a type-approval certificate and vehicle equipment issued by another state.

(2) The district authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 10,000,000 on a person who produced and placed on the market a road vehicle without technical fitness approval.

(3) The Ministry or the district authority, within the scope of its competence, shall impose a fine of up to CZK 5,000,000 on a person who altered identification data on essential parts of the mechanism or construction of a road vehicle.

(4) The Ministry shall impose a fine of up to CZK 5,000,000 on a person who violated the conditions stipulated in the decision on type-approval of technical fitness.

(5) The district authority shall impose a fine of up to CZK 500,000 on a person who

- a) operates a technical inspection station or an emission measurement station without a valid authorization from the district authority or carries out technical inspections of road vehicles before their technical fitness for operation on roads is approved without a permit,

- b) fails to comply with the conditions stipulated by this Act and the implementing legal regulation for the operation of a technical inspection station or an emission measurement station.

(6) In case of a breach of obligations stipulated in § 77 (3) and (4), the Czech Trade Inspection Authority shall decide on measures or impose a fine in accordance with special legal regulations.^13)

p84.text:
(1) The Ministry, the Czech Trade Inspection Authority, or the district authority shall impose a fine within one year from the day the breach of obligations was ascertained, and no later than within three years from the day the breach of obligations occurred, and if it concerns the ongoing unauthorized operation of a technical inspection station or an emission measurement station, no later than within five years from the time the unauthorized activity was still ongoing.

(2) Fines imposed by the district authority shall be collected and enforced by the district authority. Fines imposed by the Ministry shall be collected and enforced by the Ministry.

(3) Fines imposed and collected under this Act shall be revenue of the state budget.

p85.text:
(1) Proceedings in matters regulated by this Act shall be conducted in accordance with the Administrative Procedure Code, unless this Act provides otherwise.

(2) If an application for a registration certificate and a certificate of type-approval of technical fitness of a road vehicle, vehicle system, vehicle component, or separate technical unit of a vehicle, a professional certificate of a control technician, a professional certificate of a control technician for issuing technical protocols of vehicles before their approval for operation on roads, a professional certificate of a mechanic, and a certificate of approval of a measuring instrument or device used for carrying out technical inspections and emission measurements, submitted under this Act, is fully granted, a certificate shall be issued instead of an administrative decision.

p86.text: The provisions of this Act shall apply only if an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound does not provide otherwise.

p87.text: For acts of the Ministry and the district authority stipulated by a special legal regulation^6), administrative fees shall be collected. Other acts of the Ministry and the district authority shall be performed for a charge, the type and amount of which shall be stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

p88.text:
(1) Manufacturers of vehicles, their systems, components, separate technical units or equipment of road vehicles are obliged to provide the Ministry with data on manufactured vehicles stipulated by an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound.

(2) The Ministry ensures the issuance of the Transport Bulletin, in which the regulatory framework and an information document for the purpose of deciding on the approval of the technical fitness of a type of road vehicle and instructions specifying the uniformity of performing technical inspections and emission measurements at technical inspection stations and emission measurement stations are published.

p89.heading: Transitional Provisions
p89.text:
(1) Sanctions for unlawful acts under this Act may only be imposed for unlawful acts that occurred after the effective date of this Act.

(2) The operator of a technical inspection station and the operator of an emission measurement station who operate a technical inspection station or an emission measurement station on the basis of a valid authorization issued under legal regulations valid before the effective date of this Act are obliged to notify the relevant district authority of the data and submit the documents required for granting authorization to operate a technical inspection station or an emission measurement station no later than one year from the effective date of this Act. If the operator fails to fulfill this obligation, the existing authorization shall cease to be valid upon the expiration of the last day of the period stipulated for notifying the data and submitting the documents. If the operator, on the basis of the notified data and submitted documents, fails to meet the conditions stipulated by this Act for granting authorization to operate a technical inspection station or an emission measurement station, the district authority shall immediately initiate proceedings for the revocation of the authorization to operate a technical inspection station or an emission measurement station.

(3) The registration of road vehicles in the records of the traffic inspectorate of the Police of the Czech Republic carried out before the effective date of this Act shall be considered registration under this Act.

(4) Vehicle technical certificates, vehicle technical attestations, technical certificate attestations, technical attestation extracts issued under legal regulations valid until the effective date of this Act shall be considered vehicle technical certificates, vehicle technical attestations, technical certificate attestations, technical attestation extracts issued under this Act.

(5) Operators of road motor vehicles and trailers are obliged to bring the data in the road vehicle register maintained under this Act into a state corresponding to reality within three years from the effective date of this Act; as regards the creation, change or termination of pledge rights, operators of road motor vehicles and trailers are obliged to bring the data in the road vehicle register into a state corresponding to reality within six months from the effective date of this Act.

(6) Technical inspection and emission measurement performed before the effective date of this Act, recorded in the vehicle technical certificate and in the emission measurement certificate and marked with a control sticker on the license plate, placed on the rear of the vehicle, shall be considered technical inspection and emission measurement under this Act.

(7) Certificates of professional competence for performing technical controls, technical inspections or for emission measurements issued before the effective date of this Act shall be considered professional certificates issued under this Act.

(8) Certificates of approval of the technical fitness of a type of road vehicle, a type of other vehicle and accessories, their parts and supplements, including their annexes, issued before the effective date of this Act shall be considered certificates issued under this Act.

(9) Decisions issued by the traffic inspectorate of the Police of the Czech Republic on the approval of the technical fitness of an individual vehicle issued before the effective date of this Act shall be considered decisions issued under this Act.

(10) Vehicles that were not subject to registration under legal regulations valid until the effective date of this Act must be registered in the road vehicle register no later than one year from the effective date of this Act.

(11) Certificates of approval of measuring instruments or equipment used for performing technical inspections and for emission measurements issued before the effective date of this Act shall be considered certificates issued under this Act.

p90.text:
(1) Rights and obligations arising from the employment relationships of employees of the Ministry of the Interior who, as of the effective date of this Act, perform activities in the area of conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads under this Act, shall transfer from the Ministry of the Interior to the Ministry of Transport and Communications as of the effective date of this Act.

(2) Rights and obligations arising from the employment relationships of employees of the Police of the Czech Republic who, as of the effective date of this Act, perform activities in the area of conditions for the operation of vehicles on roads under this Act, shall transfer from the Police of the Czech Republic to the district authorities in whose territorial district these employees have their workplaces as of the effective date of this Act.

p91.heading: Final Provisions
p91.text:
(1) The Ministry shall issue a decree to implement § 2 (5) to (8), § 4 (7), § 5 (3), § 7 (2) and (4), § 18 (4), § 19 (5) and (8), § 25 (2), § 31 (1) and (2), § 33 (2), § 36 (2) and (3), § 44 (6), § 45 (1), (5) and (6), § 47 (4), § 48 (1) and (4), § 53 (2), § 54 (6), § 57 (3), § 58 (1) (b) and (c), § 60 (3), § 62 (2), § 63 (5), § 66 (3), § 67 (1) (b) and (c), § 69 (3), § 71 (2), § 72 (3), § 74 (1), § 76 (1), § 78 (1) and (3), § 79 (5), § 83 (5) (b) and § 87.

(2) The Ministry of Industry and Trade shall issue a decree to implement § 77 (2) to (5).

(3) The Ministry of the Environment shall issue a decree to implement § 37 (b).

(4) The Ministry of Transport and Communications, in agreement with the Ministry of the Environment and the Ministry of Agriculture, shall issue a decree to implement § 78 (3).

(5) Manufacturers of road vehicles, vehicle systems, vehicle components and separate technical units of vehicles, special vehicles and other persons involved in the approval or recognition of type technical competence are obliged to comply with technical regulations issued

- a) on the basis of an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound,^7) issued by the United Nations Economic Commission for Europe,

- b) on the basis of the European Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road (ADR),^14)

- c) on the basis of the European Agreement concerning the Work of Crews of Vehicles engaged in International Road Transport (AETR),^15)

- d) pursuant to Article 7 of the Europe Agreement establishing an association between the Czech Republic, of the one part, and the European Communities and their Member States, of the other part,^11)

in the wording adopted by the Czech Republic. These technical regulations shall be published in the Transport Bulletin and shall be available at the Ministry.

p92.text:
The following are repealed:

- 1. Act No. 38/1995 Sb., on the technical conditions for the operation of road vehicles on land communications.

- 2. Act No. 355/1999 Sb., amending Act No. 38/1995 Sb., on the technical conditions for the operation of road vehicles on land communications.

p93.text:
Act No. 168/1999 Sb., on liability insurance for damage caused by the operation of a vehicle and on amendment of certain related acts (Vehicle Operation Liability Insurance Act), as amended by Act No. 307/1999 Sb., is amended as follows:

- 1. In § 2 (b), the words „is subject to record-keeping“ are replaced by the words „is subject to registration“.

- 2. Footnote No. 9) reads:

„^9) § 4 to 6 of Act No. 56/2001 Sb., on the conditions for the operation of vehicles on land communications and on amendment of Act No. 168/1999 Sb., on liability insurance for damage caused by the operation of a vehicle and on amendment of certain related acts (Vehicle Operation Liability Insurance Act), as amended by Act No. 307/1999 Sb.“.

- 3. In § 15 (2), the words „from the central vehicle record“ are repealed.

- 4. In § 15 (3), the word „Interior“ is replaced by the words „Transport and Communications“.

- 5. In § 15 (4), the word „Interior“ is replaced by the words „Transport and Communications“.

- 6. In § 15 (9), after the words „de-registration of the vehicle“, the words „from the register^23)“ are inserted, and footnote No. 23), which reads:

„^23) § 13 and 14 of Act No. 56/2001 Sb.“.

p94.heading: Effectiveness
p94.text:
This Act shall enter into force on 1 July 2001.

sprilohakzakonuc5.text:
Vehicle Categories

- A) Basic Vehicle Categories

Category L - motor vehicles generally with fewer than four wheels

Category M - motor vehicles with at least four wheels, used for the carriage of persons

Category N - motor vehicles with at least four wheels, used for the carriage of goods

Category O - trailers

Category T - agricultural or forestry tractors

Category S - work machines

Category R - other vehicles that cannot be classified into the above categories

- B) Subdivision of certain vehicle categories

(1) Vehicle category L is subdivided into

mopeds

- a) two-wheel mopeds are two-wheel vehicles with an engine cylinder capacity not exceeding 50 cm^3 in the case of an internal combustion engine and with a maximum design speed not exceeding 45 kmh^-1 for any type of propulsion,

- b) three-wheel mopeds are three-wheel vehicles with any wheel arrangement, with an engine cylinder capacity not exceeding 50 cm^3 in the case of an internal combustion engine and with a maximum design speed not exceeding 45 kmh^-1 for any type of propulsion,

- c) light quadricycles, whose unladen mass is less than 350 kg, not including the mass of batteries in the case of electric vehicles, and whose maximum design speed does not exceed 45 kmh^-1 and whose engine cylinder capacity does not exceed 50 cm^3 for spark-ignition engines or, for other types of engines, whose maximum net power does not exceed 4 kW,

motorcycles

- a) motorcycles are two-wheel vehicles with an engine cylinder capacity exceeding 50 cm^3 in the case of an internal combustion engine, or with a maximum design speed exceeding 45 kmh^-1 for any type of propulsion,

- b) motorcycles with a sidecar are vehicles with three wheels arranged unsymmetrically with respect to the median longitudinal plane, with an engine cylinder capacity exceeding 50 cm^3 in the case of an internal combustion engine, or with a maximum design speed exceeding 45 kmh^-1 for any type of propulsion,

motor tricycles

- a) motor tricycles are vehicles with three wheels arranged symmetrically with respect to the median longitudinal plane with an engine cylinder capacity exceeding 50 cm^3 in the case of an internal combustion engine, or with a maximum design speed exceeding 45 kmh^-1 for any type of propulsion,

- b) quadricycles other than light quadricycles, whose unladen mass does not exceed 400 kg or 550 kg for vehicles intended for the carriage of goods, not including the mass of batteries in the case of electric vehicles, and whose maximum net engine power does not exceed 15 kW,

power-assisted cycle

a bicycle with a permanently installed engine with an engine cylinder capacity not exceeding 50 cm^3 in the case of an internal combustion engine and with a maximum design speed not exceeding 25 kmh^-1 for any type of propulsion.

Vehicles classified under ECE - UN in categories L1 and L2 with a maximum design speed of 50 kmh^-1 are considered mopeds, vehicles in categories L3 and L4 are considered motorcycles, and vehicles in category L5 are considered motor tricycles.

(2) Vehicle category M is subdivided into

- a) M1 - vehicles with no more than eight seats for passengers, in addition to the driver's seat, or multi-purpose vehicles,

- b) M2 - vehicles with more than eight seats for passengers, in addition to the driver's seat, and whose maximum permissible mass does not exceed 5,000 kg,

- c) M3 - vehicles with more than eight seats for passengers, in addition to the driver's seat, and whose maximum permissible mass exceeds 5,000 kg.

(3) A „multi-purpose vehicle“ is a vehicle with a body type designed for the carriage of persons and goods in a single compartment of the vehicle. The classification of vehicles by body type and the conditions for classifying a multi-purpose vehicle into vehicle category M1 or N1 are stipulated by an implementing legal regulation.

(4) When deciding on the classification of a vehicle into category M and N, a place for a sitting, lying, or standing person, or a place with permanent seat anchorage points, is considered a „place for the carriage of persons“, regardless of whether a seat is or is not installed in that place.

(5) Vehicle category N is subdivided into

- a) N1 - a vehicle whose maximum permissible mass does not exceed 3,500 kg,

- b) N2 - a vehicle whose maximum permissible mass exceeds 3,500 kg but does not exceed 12,000 kg,

- c) N3 - a vehicle whose maximum permissible mass exceeds 12,000 kg.

(6) An „off-road vehicle“ is a motor vehicle with increased passability, belonging to vehicle category M or N and meeting other technical conditions stipulated by an implementing legal regulation. An off-road vehicle of the respective category is designated by the supplementary letter G to category M or N, for example, M1G, N3G.

(7) Vehicle category O is subdivided into

- a) O1 - trailers whose maximum permissible mass does not exceed 750 kg,

- b) O2 - trailers whose maximum permissible mass exceeds 750 kg but does not exceed 3,500 kg,

- c) O3 - trailers whose maximum permissible mass exceeds 3,500 kg but does not exceed 10,000 kg,

- d) O4 - trailers whose maximum permissible mass exceeds 10,000 kg,

- e) OT1 - tractor trailers whose maximum permissible mass does not exceed 1,500 kg,

- f) OT2 - tractor trailers whose maximum permissible mass exceeds 1,500 kg but does not exceed 3,500 kg,

- g) OT3 - tractor trailers whose maximum permissible mass exceeds 3,500 kg but does not exceed 6,000 kg,

- h) OT4 - tractor trailers whose maximum permissible mass exceeds 6,000 kg.

(8) Vehicle category S is subdivided into

- a) Ss - a „self-propelled work machine“ is a special vehicle with its own power source, designed and equipped solely for performing specific work activities. A self-propelled work machine is generally not intended for transport activities,

- b) SP - a „trailer work machine“ is a special vehicle without its own power source, designed and equipped solely for performing specific work activities. A trailer work machine is attached to a towing motor vehicle adapted for its attachment. A trailer work machine is generally not intended for transport activities. Trailer work machines are divided into

- 1. SP1 trailer work machine with a maximum technically permissible mass not exceeding 3,000 kg,

- 2. SP2 trailer work machine with a technically permissible mass exceeding 3,000 kg but not exceeding 6,000 kg,

- 3. SP3 work machine with a technically permissible mass exceeding 6,000 kg.
